title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the net force on the shot, we need to use the formula for force: $$F=ma$$, where F is the force, $$m$$ is the mass, and a is the acceleration.

title: Finding Acceleration
text: We can find the acceleration using the kinematic equation: $$v^2=u^2+2a s$$, where v is the final velocity, u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and s is the distance.

title: Initial and Final Velocity
text: Since the shot is initially at rest, the initial velocity $$u=0$$. The final velocity v can be found using the range formula for projectile motion.

title: Projectile Motion Formula
text: The range R of a projectile is given by $$R=\frac{v^2 sin\left(2\theta\right)}{g}$$, where v is the initial velocity, theta is the angle of projection, and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Calculate Initial Velocity
text: Using the range formula, solve for the initial velocity v. Given: $$R=22.22$$ $$m$$, $$theta=45$$ degrees, $$g=9.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.

title: Calculate Acceleration
text: Now, use the kinematic equation $$v^2=u^2+2a s$$ to find the acceleration a. Given: $$v=14.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, $$u=0$$, $$s=1.20$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Net Force
text: Finally, use the formula $$F=ma$$ to find the net force. Given: $$m=7.26$$ kg, $$a=91.1$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.
